course.title: TypeScript: A Practical Beginner Guide
course.description: Master TypeScript from the ground up with hands-on projects and real-world examples. This beginner-level course takes you from core concepts to production-ready code.

lessons.title: Introduction to TypeScript
lessons.objective: Understand what TypeScript is, why it matters, and set up your development environment

data.text: Alright, welcome to Introduction to TypeScript. In this lesson we're going to get hands-on with TypeScript. By the end you'll have a solid understanding of the core concepts, and you'll have written some real code.
Let's start simple. Here's your first TypeScript program. Don't worry if it looks unfamiliar — we'll break down every single line.
Now let's level it up a notch. We'll add some structure using interfaces and classes. This is how real TypeScript code looks in production.

data.label: First Code Check
data.description: Make sure your greet function returns the correct string when called with your name

data.text: Excellent work! You've just built a working TypeScript structure. Take a moment to experiment — change the values, break things intentionally, and see what happens. That's how you really learn.

data.label: Class Exercise
data.description: Complete the exercise: extend App with task management

codeExamples.explanation: This example demonstrates the basic structure of a TypeScript program. We define a typed function 'greet' that takes a string parameter and returns a greeting. The backtick template literal allows embedding expressions inside strings using ${}.

Here we introduce interfaces and classes — core OOP concepts. The Config interface defines the shape of our configuration object. The App class uses a constructor with TypeScript's shorthand property declaration (private config: Config) to automatically create and assign a class property.

narrationSegments.text: Alright, welcome to Introduction to TypeScript. In this lesson we're going to get hands-on with TypeScript. By the end you'll have a solid understanding of the core concepts, and you'll have written some real code.
Let's start simple. Here's your first TypeScript program. Don't worry if it looks unfamiliar — we'll break down every single line.
Now let's level it up a notch. We'll add some structure using interfaces and classes. This is how real TypeScript code looks in production.
Excellent work! You've just built a working TypeScript structure. Take a moment to experiment — change the values, break things intentionally, and see what happens. That's how you really learn.

lessons.title: Core Concepts & Syntax
lessons.objective: Master the fundamental syntax and core concepts of TypeScript

lessons.title: Functions & Modularity
lessons.objective: Write reusable, modular code using functions and modules
